members.name: Julia (DE)
members.background: Public Health, Physiotherapist, B.A. Social Pedagogics
members.peopleAskMeFor:
- Dealing with other humans in any way (networking, team management, outreach, presenting on stage, ...)

- connecting seemingly opposing things
- puzzles, connecting multiple tasks & keeping an overview, fixing physical devices/build ikea furniture
members.naturalExecutionTask:
- present something
- make sure everyone is happy with their part and can fulfill their potential (listen to people, help them find their happy place, focus and passion)
- connect their tasks to align the common goal (talk to people, transfer ideas, create group spirit, remind people of the common goal)
- message people, reach out to stakeholders
- help create physical things
members.funResponsibility:
- speak on stage / lead a workshop
- steer the project and keep different teams aligned to the common goal, making sure everyone spends their time most efficiently on the project
- (Reaching out to stakeholders)
- do some creative physical work (like painting a big canvas for the stage/building something physical)
members.drainers:
- digital design (never works as planned)
- repetitive operational work
- buying things
- too many tabs, open files, docs, mails, digital chaos
- unfruitful, endless discussions
members.constraints:
Starting next week: Master thesis with patient data, so I have to be in Dresden for that.
New remote job 8h/week from May on.
members.ownedSlice: Be on stage & on-site experience, setting the vision and steering the project
members.driverSeatWhy: I like to be in the driver's seat, but time may be constricting me before the event. As well, who us ever fully ready? :)
members.otherIncentive: Inspiration on HOW to make the world a better place, while leading a wholesome life.
members.personalWin:
Career boost on global stage and practice of wholesome living:
- Very valuable new and lasting contacts
- Being invited to great opportunities as a speaker & thought leader.
- being content with my own achievements and happily driven to continue reaching for the stars
members.hatWhy: I'd love to shape the vision and define the direction. Recently I led a team of 30 medicine-students to ideate and build a hardware tech device (as a spring school challenge). It was very fun to be the one with an overview on everything, defining the common goal, keeping up the communication between different departments, minding the deadlines and steering the whole project to a successful common goal. It was challenging, that none of us really knew about hardware tech, but we successfully delivered a prototype better than the one made by the competing engineering students. :)

members.name: Harshita Sharma
members.background: Materials Science and Simulations, Buisness, Design Thinking
members.peopleAskMeFor: organizing stuff, tech tools and group vibes....
members.feelsLikePlay: managing time doing multiple things, working on a clear white sheet and building on top of vague ideas.....
members.naturalExecutionTask:
I can take care of managing tasks and building a clear strategy on the next steps.....
also somehow algining the taks with in the whole group so that everyone is contributing something and they also feel accomplished....
members.funResponsibility: Communication, talking to people, be on stage with some fun elements, not only fun for me but for others also, though this is not my comfort zone, but still want to do it to break my imposter syndrome.....
members.drainers: endless conversations and just surface level interest but in real have no exceitment for the work.....
members.constraints: Have a full time job, working on my own start-up idea, taking out some time for Gym, cooking, working......
members.driverSeatWhy:
I’m ready to take full ownership, as these are the kinds of opportunities we rarely get. Even though my schedule is quite tight, I’m up for it.

That said, I don’t want to be in the driver’s seat alone — a strong team always creates a much bigger impact. I’m really looking for a setup where there’s shared motivation, genuine interest, and, most importantly, some fun in building something great together.
members.otherIncentive: For me, it’s also about personal growth — getting to know myself better and building genuine, meaningful connections along the way.
members.personalWin:
For me, it would feel like a real win if, as a team, we manage to deliver something truly creative and out of the box — something that initially feels almost impossible: extremely challenging, uncomfortable, maybe even a bit weird — but we still pull it off.

In the end, I’d love to surprise myself too.
members.hatWhy:
I’m not completely sure yet, but I know I’d really like to work closely with people. You get to understand others better when you talk, coordinate, and collaborate — everyone is different, and that’s what makes it interesting.

I’d love to work with all kinds of people, listen to their perspectives, and share experiences along the way while building something meaningful together. Isn’t that what makes it great?

members.name: Leon
members.peopleAskMeFor: “Can we look at this differently?” “How can we hack reality with this?” “Where’s the unfair advantage here?” Turning chaos into a clear system or framework Sharpening messages, offers, and stories so they click
members.feelsLikePlay: - Decomposing messy problems into steps, flows, and systems - Connecting weird dots between art, tech, business, and people - Sitting in long 1:1s, asking questions, and reframing someone’s situation - Designing tools and workflows that collapse effort (content systems, robots, processes) - Radical Decomposition - Thinking about how to kick in the dore, and pull all levers I can get hands on
members.naturalExecutionTask: I open a doc or whiteboard, map the whole thing as a system, define lanes, and then start building the first working version (tool, script, content flow) so people can plug into something real.
members.funResponsibility: Owning the “thinking + systems + story” side: designing the overall flow of the youth intervention and the Team Map / documentation so everyone’s work becomes visible and reusable.
members.drainers: - Endless open discussions with no decision or owner - Vague tasks like “someone should…” - Last‑minute chaos that could have been avoided with basic planning - Pure surface‑level “vibes” with no real outcome
members.constraints: No exams. Balancing some freelance/content work and system‑building, but calendar is flexible as long as roles are clear and we don’t decide everything 24h before. I'm not up for working through the night currently...
members.ownedSlice: Owning the Team Map + content & documentation system for the youth intervention: How we capture what happens, and how we turn it into proof and assets for HOBB, YETI, and the team.
members.driverSeatWhy: I’m ready to drive on systems, content, and structure, but I don’t want to be “CEO of everything.” I need co‑owners on ops/logistics and stakeholder stuff so I can stay zoomed out on the bigger picture and keep all the moving parts aligned. I spent the last 6 months building a ready‑to‑deploy content system that lets us create set-and-forget style 1 reel in about 102 seconds. I can contribute this as media leverage for the whole youth team.
members.otherIncentive: One or two deep relationships out of this – with people I’d genuinely build with again (co‑founder, long‑term collaborator, or mentor). I built alone most of the time, its more fun to escape boredom together, isn't it?
members.personalWin: Looking back in 6 months, a win would be: - We shipped a youth intervention at HOBB that people still talk about. Their life gained more quality through beauty and empathy that got productised. - I have a clear story + proof of how my system‑thinking and content tools made the project better. - I leave with 2–3 people I trust and want to build with again, plus something I can put in my portfolio as “this is what I do in the world.”
members.hatWhy:
I wear whatever hat is needed to push the team forward.

The sweet spot for me is: co-design the game, co‑lead it, and join then strong ops people who fly with me inside it.

members.name: Etkin Celep
members.background: Physics Engineering student at Istanbul Technical University. Founder of a student-led organization building scalable support systems for students (itüöder). Experience across marketing, business development, and creative production.
members.peopleAskMeFor: Structuring ideas into executable projects Building and scaling communities Partnerships, sponsorships, and business development Content strategy and storytelling Making messy things clear and actionable
members.feelsLikePlay: Turning vague ideas into real, working systems Designing projects from zero to execution Connecting people and creating opportunities between them Writing messages, pitches, and positioning ideas clearly Thinking about how things can scale and sustain themselves
members.naturalExecutionTask: I usually structure the work and make it actionable — define next steps, set up a simple system (Notion/flow), and start the first version (doc, message, or concept) so the team has something real to react to.
members.funResponsibility: Owning a part of the project end-to-end — especially where I can shape the direction and turn it into something real and visible with the team.
members.drainers: Long discussions without clear decisions or next steps Purely operational repetitive tasks with no ownership or impact Unclear roles where responsibility is diffused
members.constraints: Final year of university + ongoing projects, so time is limited but flexible when there is clear ownership and impact
members.ownedSlice: Creative direction — how we show up visually, what story we tell, and how we make the youth voice land with impact. This includes social media, but also the on-site experience: how things look, feel, and get remembered. I want to own the thread that connects everything we create into one coherent identity.
members.driverSeatWhy: I’m comfortable taking ownership and turning ideas into execution, especially in content and strategy. The only reason it’s not 10 is time constraints, but when I take something on, I make sure it moves forward and becomes real.
members.personalWin: I want to leave Athens with at least two or three real connections who remember me for what I brought, not just that I was there. And I want a piece of work I'm genuinely proud to show — something that proves I can take an idea from concept to execution under real pressure.
members.hatWhy: I naturally think in terms of direction, structure, and how things should come together end-to-end. In my past projects, I’ve been most effective when defining the approach and then turning it into something real with the team, rather than just executing predefined tasks.
